6 Largest Mainline Fleets In North America

Home to some of the largest airlines, North America is a region of high airline activity. Which of the regions airlines have the biggest fleets?

Fleet size concentration among six carriers demonstrates North America's oligopolistic market structure, with significant implications for route competition and pricing power.

The Simple Flying Podcast Episode 285: Delta Air Lines' New High-Speed WiFi, FAA Bans SFO's Parallel Landings

Join Tom & Channing for the latest aviation hot topics.

Delta's WiFi upgrade addresses passenger connectivity demands while SFO's parallel landing suspension indicates potential safety concerns requiring operational restructuring.

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

Stopping Power: Boeing 777-9 Brakes Reach 2,500 Degrees In Critical Aborted Takeoff Test

The aircraft pushes the limits of its advanced braking system during a simulated maximum-weight, high-speed aborted takeoff.

Boeing's 777-9 brakes withstand extreme 2,500-degree thermal stress during maximum-weight abort scenarios, validating critical safety margins for the largest twin-engine widebody.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers

NASA’s first Artemis II science officers—Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia—are pioneering a new era of mission operations by integrating advanced lunar science directly into Mission Control. Their work will shape how future Artemis crews observe, analyze, and deepen our understanding of...

Dedicated science officers in Mission Control will enable real-time lunar observation analysis and adaptive mission strategies, fundamentally changing how crews conduct in-situ scientific work.

See NASA →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand

Italy’s Argotec has officially opened its first U.S. satellite production facility, cementing a foothold near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to join other foreign space firms pursuing growing demand from American programs.

European space manufacturers are establishing U.S. production hubs to capture defense and commercial satellite contracts, signaling a shift toward distributed manufacturing in the aerospace supply chain.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Apr 3, 2026

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Organizations with strong psychological safety see higher hazard reporting rates, enabling proactive risk mitigation before incidents occur.

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

Why In The World Did Emirates Buy Another Airbus A380 Last Year?

The airline remains committed to the type for the next decade or so.

Emirates' continued A380 investment signals confidence in ultra-high-capacity aircraft demand for premium routes despite industry-wide shift toward smaller widebodies.

6 Military Aircraft With The Highest Operating Costs

The priciest warplanes in the sky in 2026.

Understanding the cost-per-flight-hour drivers of high-end military aircraft—such as fuel consumption, maintenance complexity, and crew requirements—offers valuable benchmarking insights for commercial operators managing similarly advanced platforms.

Military Apr 3, 2026

US F-15E Shot Down Over Iran, One Crew Member Rescued

The fate of the aircraft’s second crew member is unknown.

Loss of a modern fighter-bomber over hostile territory underscores the persistent air defense threat from regional adversaries and recovery operation complexities.

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern aviation SMS shifts operators from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen.

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

FAA report proposes sweeping rewrite of Part 141 flight training rules

A 471-page industry report released through the FAA’s Part 141 modernization docket proposes a sweeping rewrite of how…...

The comprehensive 471-page proposal signals the FAA's willingness to fundamentally restructure flight training regulations, potentially opening pathways for faster certification and alternative training methodologies.

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

Inside The Boeing 787 Dreamliner's Crew Rest Area

Step inside the hidden world of Boeing 787's crew rest areas, a space that remains a mystery to most passengers.

The 787's upper-deck crew rest compartment reduces fatigue-related errors on ultra-long-haul flights by allowing pilots and flight attendants to rotate off-duty rest periods during 16+ hour operations.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

5 Hidden Features Of The Airbus A350 Cabin Design

Airbus has designed a quiet, high-humidity, and comfortably pressurized cabin for its flagship modern widebody aircraft.

The A350's cabin engineering optimizes three physiological factors simultaneously—noise reduction, humidity retention, and pressurization—to measurably reduce passenger fatigue on long-haul routes.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Apr 3, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit geometry at LAX creates systematic confusion for pilots transitioning from Runway 24R, indicating that physical infrastructure design—not just procedural compliance—drives incursion risk.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom

Today marks the 100th anniversary of the birth of Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom, born April 3, 1926, in Mitchell, Indiana. As one of NASA’s first seven astronauts, he became America’s second astronaut to fly in space when he launched aboard the Liberty Bell 7 spacecraft on July 21, 1961, just weeks after...

Grissom's July 1961 suborbital flight demonstrated rapid American capability to execute human spaceflight just weeks after Shepard's initial success, establishing momentum for the Mercury program's competitive advantage during the Space Race.

See NASA →

Military Apr 3, 2026

Picture of the Day: April 3, 2026

A C-130J awaits its next assignment on the flightline.

The C-130J's continued operational presence demonstrates the enduring reliability of this transport platform across decades of military service missions.

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

ZeroAvia edges closer to cert as FAA issues electric engine conditions

ZeroAvia has this week celebrated a “major step” towards certification of its ZA600 hydrogen-electric powertrain, after the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) published special conditions to certify the system’s electric engine component. The FAA published 33 special conditions for...

FAA's 33 special conditions establish the regulatory framework for hydrogen-electric powertrains, accelerating industry standardization beyond ZeroAvia's individual certification path.

See Runway Girl Network →

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures must balance operational efficiency with residential constraints in one of the nation's most congested airspace regions.

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

White House again proposes steep NASA budget cuts

For the second consecutive year, the White House is proposing a major budget cut for NASA that would significantly impact the agency’s science programs and the International Space Station.

Sustained NASA budget reductions threaten aerospace supply chain stability and commercial space partnerships that depend on ISS operations and government contracts.

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Test Pilots and the Road Back to the Moon

For NASA’s first crewed lunar mission since Apollo, aviation experience is one of the clearest threads linking the astronauts aboard Orion.

NASA's Artemis lunar program prioritizes pilots with extensive aviation backgrounds, recognizing that atmospheric flight experience provides critical skills for spacecraft operations and contingency management.

See AVweb →

Industry Apr 3, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation layers offers immediate efficiency gains while emerging technologies mature, addressing capacity constraints and environmental pressures simultaneously.

See MDPI →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

United Airlines Slashes 26% Of Domestic Widebody Flights: Here's Why

An increasing number of 777-200s are being sent to the desert.

United's widebody reduction signals a structural shift toward narrowbody efficiency as carriers reassess long-haul domestic profitability in a slower demand environment.

Exclusive: Canadians Are Skipping The US – And This Airline Is Cashing In

CEO Adam Scott sat down with Simple Flying to discuss how the flag carrier of Bermuda is preparing for its third summer season.

Bermuda's flag carrier is capitalizing on Canadian travelers avoiding US connections by expanding its summer network for a third consecutive year.

DJI is continuing to refine its mapping and modeling ecosystem with the latest update to DJI Terra, and version 5.2.0 brings a mix of speed boosts, smarter automation, and improved output quality for enterprise users. more…...

DJI's performance optimization focus reflects the drone industry's shift toward real-time enterprise applications where processing speed directly impacts operational efficiency and competitive positioning.

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Hello, World

NASA astronaut and Artemis II Commander Reid Wiseman took this picture of Earth from the Orion spacecraft’s window after completing the translunar injection burn. There are two auroras (top right and bottom left) and zodiacal light (bottom right) is visible as the Earth eclipses the Sun. This and...

Artemis II's successful translunar injection demonstrates NASA's operational readiness for lunar return missions, with real-time imagery validating Orion spacecraft performance at critical mission phases.

US drone future hinges on policy, investment, coordination

The Commercial Drone Alliance is pushing Washington to move faster, and more strategically, on building a truly domestic drone ecosystem. In a newly released white paper, the industry group calls on the Trump Administration to adopt sweeping reforms aimed at strengthening US manufacturing, creating...

Industry fragmentation and regulatory delays are threatening US competitiveness in drone manufacturing against foreign competitors who face fewer bureaucratic barriers.

SpaceX and Amazon spar over satellite deployments

Amazon says it will revise deployment plans for its broadband satellite constellation while denying claims from SpaceX that its current approach represents a space safety risk.

Orbital debris management disputes between commercial satellite operators could influence future regulatory frameworks for mega-constellation deployments.

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

DARPA’s X-65 Active Flow Control Demonstrator is Taking Shape

Aurora Flight Sciences and DARPA are continuing the assembly of the X-65 CRANE demonstrator, targeting a first flight by 2027. Aurora Flight Sciences’ (AFS) latest X-Plane, now designated the X-65, is taking its final shape. The company unveiled the fuselage of the unmanned demonstrator, which uses...

Active flow control technology could reduce aircraft drag by 5-8%, enabling significant fuel efficiency gains and lower operating costs across commercial aviation fleets.

Boeing-Built Core Stage Powers Historic Artemis II Launch

The 212-foot rocket component operated ‘exactly as intended’ during around-the-moon launch...

Boeing's successful Artemis II core stage performance validates the Space Launch System architecture for sustained lunar exploration, establishing a proven foundation for future deep-space missions.

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Europe’s strategic autonomy in space will define its role in the ‘second space age’

Europe’s future in space really boils down to one question: can it stay ahead without relying on technology made somewhere else? As we step into what experts call the “second space age,” strategic autonomy is suddenly front and center for the European Union. And it is increasingly essential that...

European aerospace competitiveness depends on developing indigenous space capabilities rather than relying on external technology partnerships, fundamentally reshaping the continent's industrial strategy.

Embraer delivers 44 aircraft in Q1 2026, up 47% year on year

Embraer delivered 44 aircraft during the first quarter of 2026, a 47% increase over the 30 units handed over in the…...

Embraer's 47% YoY delivery surge signals robust regional jet demand recovery and the company's ability to scale production amid industry capacity constraints.

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

China’s commercial Tianlong-3 rocket fails on debut launch

The first launch of the Tianlong-3 rocket from Chinese commercial firm Space Pioneer failed Friday after suffering an anomaly in its ascent phase.

China's commercial space sector faces development hurdles that could delay aerospace supply chain diversification for satellite operators seeking launch alternatives.

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

Airlines face a fuel hedging reckoning as Iran conflict reshapes flying costs

Jet fuel prices have more than doubled since the start of Operation Epic Fury, but the financial damage…...

Airlines that locked in fuel hedges before geopolitical escalation now face margin compression as spot prices surge, creating a competitive disadvantage versus hedged competitors.
